what_it_does: A research-findings markdown companion that measures whether the system can extract a trustworthy real connection graph; it quantifies the accuracy gap of the current substring-based reachability heuristic, argues a trustworthy graph is feasible on this codebase, and specifies a three-leg hybrid (AST static, registry-as-declared-truth, event-log runtime) joined across the Python/TS HTTP boundary.
observations: Frames itself as the make-or-break area where a false finding is corrosive to an autonomous loop and to Tim's trust.
Uses an evidence convention adding External-prior-art to the standard Verified/Observed/Inferred/[my idea] tags.
Reports measured numbers this run: regex route extraction == AST extraction (115=115); of 82 'wired' routes, >=3 are wired only by a mention (~3.7% false-wire rate).
Names the dangerous direction as false-WIRE (a dead route reading as whole), distinct from false-orphan.

Counts code-shape evidence: 103 + 20 distinct SUITE.<method> call-edges (bridge + MCP), zero getattr route-dispatch, 18 statically-mounted FE regions, 2106 events with 58% carrying an address, 80 ui:// entries in addresses.json.

Gives concrete false-wire examples: /api/mockup-feedback (comments only), /api/scope (a test asserting existence), /api/voice/turn (test prose), and a self-caught false positive on /api/stream (EventSource consumer it initially missed).
Presents a tool-comparison table (pyan, code2flow, vulture, pyflakes, import-linter/grimp, knip, dependency-cruiser/madge, CPG/Joern, fitness functions) with what each does/fails/adaptable.

Includes an ADDENDUM from a second independent pass adding an AST-walk-bleed finding (/api/run binding to 59 methods via naive ast.walk), a per-edge-type rigor matrix, the missed mcp_face caller surface, and a broken ui->code link.
standouts: The carry-forward number 3 of 82 (false-wire) reframing the gap as consumer-side, not extraction-side.
The directional composition rule: static proposes candidate-dead; registry and event-log may only demote; nothing declares an edge dead from absence.
The structure-aware-AST finding (naive ast.walk fabricates 59-method edges) showing the upgrade is structural fidelity, not the slogan 'AST'.
conventions: Strict evidence labeling with measured-this-run numbers privileged over theory.
Skeptical/adversarial stance explicitly requested and held ('I was asked to be rigorous, not to confirm').
Grounds external tool claims to categories the codebase already implements rather than fabricated feature claims.
concerns: The measured rates (3 of 82, 115=115) are single-run snapshots tied to the current tree state.
Some prior-art specifics (grimp/import-linter) are explicitly not retrieved and mapped by category, a stated limitation the reader must respect.
notes: The rigor argument: route extraction is already accurate but the consumer substring test silently mis-states ~3.7% of wired routes, and a trustworthy graph is feasible here only as a three-leg directional hybrid with a bespoke cross-language join.
questions: What is the ANCHOR.md framing this file partly inverts (it says the anchor frames the gap as string->AST)?
What are the sibling areas (Area 2, Area 6) it references as handing the hard part here?
Did the proposed three-leg hybrid get built, and was the resident vLLM's behavior on the join ever probed?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated deliverable (measured accuracy gap, the closing hybrid, achievable-now vs aspirational) is answered directly in section 9 and grounded with run numbers.
apparent_themes: Trustworthiness as the real deliverable for an autonomous loop
Positive-only corroboration (runtime presence proves, absence proves nothing)
Declared dynamism is analyzable; hidden metaprogramming is not
intention_signals: Trying to replace a confident framing with measured truth: prove the cheap heuristic is wrong on the consumer side, prove a trustworthy graph is buildable on this specific well-shaped codebase, and bound exactly one class (half-migration) as candidate-only.
novelty: Quantifying the false-wire gap empirically (3 of 82) and the structure-aware-AST-bleed counterexample (59 fabricated method edges) that distinguishes naive AST from trustworthy extraction.
contribution: Contributes the measured consumer-side gap, the three-leg directional hybrid design, the bespoke Python/TS route-literal join, the per-edge rigor matrix, and the bounding of half-migration as candidate-only.
summary_for_embedding: A coherence research companion measuring the substring reachability heuristic's ~3.7% false-wire rate and specifying a three-leg (AST/registry/event-log) trustworthy connection graph for this codebase.
intention_for_embedding: To determine, with measured evidence, whether a trustworthy self-extracted connection graph is feasible and what hybrid achieves it without overclaiming.
